folio.label: 91v
folio.l: But I on other wyse , may be wreke
I shal deffame hi~ , ouä al where I speke
The fals blasfemour , that charged me
To parte , that wyl nat , departed be
To euäy man I liche , witú meschaunce
The lord sat stille , as he were in a traunce
And in his herte , he rolletú vp and doun
How that this cherl had , ymaginaciouö
To shewe swicú a probleme , to the frere
Neuäe erst er now , ne herd I swicú matere
I trowe the deuele , put it in his mynde
In ars metryk , shal there noman fynde
Byforn~ this day , of swicú a questiouö
Who shulde make , a demonstraciouö
That euäy man , shuld han lyke his part
As of a soun , or of a sauour of a fart
O nyce proud cherl , I shrewe his face
Lo sires q¶ the lord , witú harde grace
To euäy man I lyke , telletú me how
Who euäe herde , of swicú a thyng or now

note.notetxt: 522521 The scribe has marked an a by 522 and a b next to 521 to indicate reversal.Est
521The line is inserted with a very dark brown the same that was used in other parts of the text, i.e. GP , and MO and the other part see the one in black ink. This shoudl be encoded somehow.ODR

folio.l: It is an impossible , it may nat be
Ey nyce cherl , god late hi~ neuäe the
The rumblynge of a fart , and euäy soune
Nis but of eyre , reuerberacione
And there it wastetú , litel and litel a wey
There nys noman , can deme be my fey
If that it were , departed equally
What lo my cherl , lo ©et how shrewedly
Vn to my confessour , to day he spak
I holde hi~ certeyn , a demonyak
Now ete ©oure mete , and late the cherl go pleye
Lat hi~ go hange hi~ self , a deuel weye
¢ Now stod the lordes squyer , at his bord
That carf his mete , and herd word by word
Of al this thyngö , of whicú I haue ©ow seyde
My lord q¶ he , be ©e©e nat euele a paide
I coude telle , for a gowne clotú
To ©ow sire frere , so ©e be nat wrotú
How that his fart , shulde euene deled be
A monge ©oure Couent , if it lyked the me
Telle q¶ the lord , & thow shalt haue a noon
A govne clotú , by god and by Seynt Ioúnö
My lord q¶ he , whan that the wedir is fayre
Witú outyn wynd , or ¹tourbyngö of eyre

note.notetxt: 546This line was added when 521 was added they are in the same colour of ink.ODR

folio.l: Lat brynge a Carte , whel , heere in to this halle
But loke that it haue , his spokes alle
Twelue spokes hatú a cart , whel , comounly
